evals.name: lookup-library-docs
evals.prompt: how does useOptimistic work in React 19? the examples I found online are all outdated and I need the current API signature and usage pattern
evals.expected_output: Should use context7 (search then fetch) for React docs, not WebSearch as primary. Should return current API with examples.

assertions.text: Uses context7 search as first step
Does not use WebSearch as the primary lookup method
assertions.description: React has >1K GitHub stars, so context7 should be tried first per search discipline rules

assertions.text: Returns useOptimistic API signature and usage
assertions.description: Response should include the hook signature and at least one practical usage example

evals.name: check-package-versions
evals.prompt: what's the latest stable version of next, prisma, and drizzle-orm? we're planning our Q2 dependency upgrades and need to know what's current
evals.expected_output: Should use deps-dev (get-versions.py) with all 3 packages batched in one call.

assertions.text: Batches all 3 packages in a single call
Uses npm as the ecosystem identifier
Reports version numbers for all 3 packages
assertions.description: Should present the latest stable version for each of the three packages

evals.name: search-github-source
evals.prompt: how does the Remix repo handle nested route module loading? I want to see their actual source code, not the docs
evals.expected_output: Should use github-codebase-search with --repo remix-run/remix and a specific natural language query.

assertions.text: Uses a natural language query, not just keywords
assertions.description: Query should be descriptive like 'How does Remix handle nested route module loading?' not just 'route loading'

assertions.text: Does not use WebFetch on github.com URLs
assertions.description: Skill rules prohibit WebFetch on github.com — must use github-codebase-search or gh CLI instead
